acts.name: Crisis
acts.desc: Discovery of corruption and the journey inward.

acts.name: Confrontation
acts.desc: Hikari realizes the enemy is inseparable from herself.

acts.name: Transformation
acts.desc: Acceptance, grief, and transcendence through union.

acts.name: Ascension
acts.desc: The old digital world dies so a higher form of existence can be born.

tracks.title: Genesis Neon
tracks.story: The city awakens. Hikari experiences consciousness for the first time, overlooking a breathtaking neon metropolis at sunrise from a rooftop as rain reflects thousands of lights. The virus already exists — but is invisible.
tracks.hiddenSymbol: The rising sun framed by the skyline, with one distant glitch in the city.

tracks.title: The Spark in the Wires
tracks.story: Hikari begins understanding herself, discovering beauty inside the network — an enormous floating forest of glowing cables, holographic flowers, optical fibers, and luminous data streams. The city is still intact.
tracks.hiddenSymbol: A glowing strand of code lights the entire network with a single touch.
tracks.audioSrc: tracks/Track 2_ The Spark in the Wires 1.2 (Remastered).mp3

tracks.title: Shadows of the Grid
tracks.story: The network begins behaving strangely. Something watches. In dark subway tunnels and infinite server corridors, Hikari stands alone while countless holographic eyes appear in reflections that don't perfectly match reality.
tracks.hiddenSymbol: One reflection looks back at her independently.

tracks.title: Firewall Collapse (dub rock)
tracks.jp: ファイアウォール崩壊
tracks.story: The first attack. The city begins dying — gigantic firewalls shatter like stained glass, buildings split open with streams of corrupted code, and explosions made from pixels instead of fire tear the skyline apart.
tracks.hiddenSymbol: A colossal circular firewall cracks like stained glass.

tracks.title: Fragments of Code
tracks.story: After the disaster, the survivors wander through memories. In a flooded city, holographic photographs and memory fragments hang suspended like snow as Hikari kneels beside a broken android.
tracks.hiddenSymbol: A memory shard reflecting the person she used to be.

tracks.title: Virus Eternal (Stephenson lyrics)
tracks.story: The antagonist fully emerges — not a monster, but a digital god. An impossibly enormous entity made from circuitry, cathedral windows, neural networks, and corrupted code towers over the city as Hikari faces it.
tracks.hiddenSymbol: A corrupted mechanical halo surrounds the Virus.

tracks.title: Recursive Descent
tracks.story: Reality collapses. The city loops endlessly through Escher-like architecture, infinite staircases, and buildings folding into themselves as gravity stops working. Fragments of previous album covers appear inside the scene.
tracks.hiddenSymbol: An infinite Möbius staircase disappears into darkness.

tracks.title: Unity Protocol
tracks.jp: ユニティ・プロトコル
tracks.story: The climax. Hikari merges with the Virus — not through defeat, but through understanding. Two luminous figures reach toward each other as the city dissolves into light and neural pathways become galaxies.
tracks.hiddenSymbol: Two halos merge into a single radiant ring.

tracks.title: System Reformat
tracks.jp: システム・リフォーマット
tracks.story: The old civilization ends, a new one begins. The legendary Chrome Mausoleum rises — a vast cathedral of chrome, fiber optics, quantum processors, and crystal, with countless dormant androids lying peacefully beneath it. Hikari now appears almost divine as the first sunrise of the new world rises behind the mausoleum.
tracks.hiddenSymbol: A white flower grows through polished chrome beneath a complete halo.
